title: Practice: Canary Deployment Strategy
description:
Implement a production-ready canary deployment with gradual traffic shifting from stable to canary version, demonstrating the complete canary rollout lifecycle.

**Focus**: Running stable and canary versions simultaneously with controlled traffic distribution based on replica counts, monitoring health, and progressively increasing canary exposure.

This exercise demonstrates the full canary pattern where a small percentage of traffic is routed to a new version (canary) while most traffic stays on the stable version. The Service selector must only include common labels (`app: nginx`) to route to both deployments. Traffic splits naturally based on replica ratios: 9 stable + 1 canary = 90/10 split. As you gain confidence in the canary's stability (monitoring error rates, latency, resource usage), you gradually scale canary up and stable down (e.g., 5:5 for 50/50, then 1:9, then 0:10 for full rollout). This progressive exposure minimizes risk compared to all-at-once deployments.
steps: Create Service that selects ONLY app=nginx (critical!)
Verify Service routes to 10 total pods (9 stable + 1 canary)
Practice scaling canary to increase traffic percentage
hints: Service selector must NOT include track or version labels
Use common label (app: nginx) in Service selector
Scale canary to 5, stable to 5 for 50/50 traffic split

editor_config.initial_code:
# Stable Deployment (90% of traffic - 9 replicas)
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: nginx-stable
spec:
  replicas: 9
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: nginx
      track: stable
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: nginx
        track: stable
        version: "1.26"
    spec:
      containers: 
        - name: nginx
          image: nginx:1.26-alpine
          ports:
            - name: http
              containerPort: 80

---
# Service (Routes to BOTH stable and canary)
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata: 
  name: nginx-canary
  labels: 
    app: nginx
spec:
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 80
      targetPort: 80
  selector: 
    app: nginx  # TODO: CRITICAL - only common label, no track/version!
    # ⚠️ Do NOT add track: stable or traffic won't reach canary
  type: NodePort

---
# Canary Deployment (10% of traffic - 1 replica)
# TODO: Create deployment with track: canary and version: "1.27"

_solution.code_solution:
# Stable Deployment (90% of traffic - 9 replicas)
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: nginx-stable
spec:
  replicas: 9  # 90% of traffic with stable version
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: nginx
      track: stable
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: nginx
        track: stable
        version: "1.26"
    spec:
      containers: 
        - name: nginx
          image: nginx:1.26-alpine
          ports:
            - name: http
              containerPort: 80

---
# Service (Routes to BOTH stable and canary)
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata: 
  name: nginx-canary
  labels: 
    app: nginx
spec:
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 80
      targetPort: 80
  selector: 
    app: nginx  # Matches BOTH stable AND canary pods
    # No 'track' label = traffic distributed to all pods
  type: NodePort

---
# Canary Deployment (10% of traffic - 1 replica)
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: nginx-canary
spec:
  replicas: 1  # 10% of traffic with canary version
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: nginx
      track: canary
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: nginx
        track: canary
        version: "1.27"
    spec:
      containers: 
        - name: nginx
          image: nginx:1.27-alpine
          ports:
            - name: http
              containerPort: 80
_solution.validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
# Apply all manifests
kubectl apply -f solution.yaml

# Wait for both deployments
timeout=120
elapsed=0
while [ $elapsed -lt $timeout ]; do
  stable_ready=$(kubectl get deployment nginx-stable -o jsonpath='{.status.readyReplicas}' 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
  canary_ready=$(kubectl get deployment nginx-canary -o jsonpath='{.status.readyReplicas}' 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
  
  if [ "$stable_ready" = "9" ] && [ "$canary_ready" = "1" ]; then
    echo "✓ Both deployments ready (stable: 9, canary: 1)"
    break
  fi
  sleep 3
  ((elapsed+=3))
done

# Verify Service selector (critical check)
selector=$(kubectl get svc nginx-canary -o jsonpath='{.spec.selector}')
if echo "$selector" | grep -q '"app":"nginx"' && ! echo "$selector" | grep -q "track"; then
  echo "✓ Service selector correct (app only, no track/version)"
  
  # Verify Service endpoints include both deployments
  endpoint_count=$(kubectl get endpoints nginx-canary -o jsonpath='{.subsets[*].addresses[*].ip}' | wc -w)
  
  if [ "$endpoint_count" -eq "10" ]; then
    echo "✓ Service has 10 endpoints (9 stable + 1 canary)"
    
    # Verify labels on both deployments
    stable_track=$(kubectl get deployment nginx-stable -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.metadata.labels.track}')
    canary_track=$(kubectl get deployment nginx-canary -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.metadata.labels.track}')
    
    if [ "$stable_track" = "stable" ] && [ "$canary_track" = "canary" ]; then
      echo "✓ Deployments have correct track labels"
      
      # Test scaling for 50/50 split
      kubectl scale deployment nginx-canary --replicas=5
      kubectl scale deployment nginx-stable --replicas=5
      sleep 15
      
      stable_scaled=$(kubectl get deployment nginx-stable -o jsonpath='{.status.readyReplicas}')
      canary_scaled=$(kubectl get deployment nginx-canary -o jsonpath='{.status.readyReplicas}')
      
      if [ "$stable_scaled" = "5" ] && [ "$canary_scaled" = "5" ]; then
        echo "✓ Successfully scaled to 50/50 traffic split"
        exit 0
      fi
    fi
  else
    echo "✗ Expected 10 endpoints, got $endpoint_count (Service selector likely includes track label)"
  fi
else
  echo "✗ Service selector incorrect (should only include app: nginx)"
  kubectl get svc nginx-canary -o yaml | grep -A 3 "selector:"
fi

echo "✗ Canary deployment validation failed"
kubectl get deployments -l app=nginx
exit 1
